RT by @hey_itsmyturn: Recent imagery indicates damage to the main maintenance hangar at Iran's Nojeh Air Base, located north of Hamedan. Additionally, several hardened aircraft shelters (HAS) currently under construction show visible changes. At this stage, these alterations could be consistent with either kinetic impacts or ongoing construction activity, requiring further assessment. Nojeh Air Base has been a recurring target during previous Israeli strike campaigns, underscoring its operational significance within Iran's air force infrastructure.

Big problem for everyone in Ukraine is that large mechanized assaults are always detected and start getting engaged way in the rear and the entire time they’re driving towards the front. So that could be 30 minutes (and in many cases much longer). So you not only need to survive the approach to the contact line, you need to survive the actual assault itself. Then you need to try and withdraw. Better to treat a lot of armor as a single-use item unless you can reliably knock down enemy UAS for some or all phases.

Israel's 🇮🇱 Mossad and Shin Bet thwarted an Iranian 🇮🇷attempt to assemble a cell to assassinate senior Israeli officials. French 🇫🇷 resident "Philip" (Thurot Ozkur) was recruited by "Babak (Baba Mahou-Zadeh Haji Jafan) and "Nedim" (Mehmet Nedim Yigit), operating on behalf of the Iranian Ministry of Intelligence, to recruit a cell of foreign nationals to infiltrate into Israel. Their handlers have been identified as Assadollah Behzad Okhsari, Vahid Masoumi, Mohammad-Hossein Zoghi, and Hamid Ghasemi. Okhsari reportedly played a central role in Iranian efforts to attack Jewish and Israeli targets worldwide. He was reportedly killed during Israel's Operation Roaring Lion. The two Iranians are linked to Iranian drug lord Naji Sharifi Zindashti, who is wanted by the US 🇺🇸 for conspiring to kill two Iranians defectors living in Maryland. Philip was reportedly expelled from France because of his involvement in planning attacks on behalf of Iran. Babak and Nedim continue to attempt to activate terror cells from Iran.

RT by @no_itsmyturn: Pay attention to this story. Some may use it as a victory lap against an increasingly flailing Iran-back terror threat. I disagree. It should be treated as a warning. Quantity (number of threats, attempts…) can have a quality of its own. Analysis of Iran-backed terror has for too long: - ignored the role of the MOIS vs IRGC - downplayed the terror threat due to foiled plots while not seeing any downturn in attempts - Not caught up to the use by Iran of lone-wolf radicals or criminal syndicates to conduct acts of subterfuge, sabotage, terror, kidnapping, assassination, and even recon vs traditional “proxies” - over-relied on allies being able to beat the Axis of Resistance as it militarized while ignoring the economic, social, political, and religious drivers the regime relies on to keep its evolving transnational terror network operating below the level where it would get upended by “mowing the grass” operations

None of these people who talk about this stuff have ever studied the Cultural Revolution, the Great Leap Forward, the Violent Struggle or anything. They don’t know what they’re talking about. They can’t name major cities. They can’t name major figures. They can’t name major factions. They can’t list slogans. They are doing this off of vibes. I really dislike people comparing Mao to Hitler or vice versa. I don’t think it results in a good conversation at all. People do it because they’re just looking to score political points rather than actually interrogating historical figures and political movements.

RT by @no_itsmyturn: This is the third time, in his second term, that President Trump publicly asks Saudi Arabia to join the Abraham Accords by bilaterally normalizing ties with Israel. Like the previous two times, I don't expect the Saudis to react to President Trump's calls (though I'd love to be proven wrong and be pleasantly surprised). If Saudi Arabia doesn't normalize ties with Israel and link up on issues of defense, the nuclear reactors will be a liability, not an asset. This is a kingdom that lives in fear that Iran might strike Saudi desalination plants. Imagine the Saudis get nuclear reactors. Their fear will multiply.

RT by @no_itsmyturn: MORE: At least seven vessels have changed course to avoid transiting through the Bab al Mandeb Strait in light of the Houthi blockade on Saudi ports. The Houthis imposed the blockade against Saudi Arabia on July 20. Six commercial vessels altered their course away from Saudi ports on July 22, according to ship-tracking data cited by Reuters. Another oil tanker that was bound for Yanbu — Saudi Arabia’s main oil export terminal on the Red Sea — turned away from the Bab al Mandeb Strait while in the Gulf of Aden on July 22. The European Union Naval Force warned commercial vessels with links to the United States, Israel, and Saudi Arabia on July 22 against sailing in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden. Commercial ship traffic through the Bab al Mandeb Strait continued on July 22 despite the Houthis’ threats against commercial vessels, however. Commercially available ship tracking data from July 22 shows that 30 commercial vessels have transited through the Bab al Mandab Strait since ISW-CTP’s last data cutoff on July 21. This is a notable decrease from the 64 commercial vessels that transited the strait between 2:00 PM ET on July 20 and 2:00 PM ET on July 21, however. At least one oil tanker transporting crude oil from Yanbu, the Xin Long Yang, initially changed course on July 21 to avoid crossing the Bab al Mandeb Strait but has begun to reapproach the strait on July 22. Houthi officials have emphasized that the Houthis will maintain their blockade on Saudi ports until Saudi Arabia lifts its blockade on Yemeni ports and airports. ISW-CTP will provide further analysis about the Houthi blockade against Saudi Arabia in its July 23 Iran Update.
